name: scrubadub
tagline: Remove personally identifiable information from free text in Python
seoTitle: scrubadub - Remove PII from Text in Python
metaDescription: scrubadub is a Python library that finds and removes personal information like names, emails, and phone numbers from free text using configurable detectors.
overview: scrubadub is a Python library that detects and removes personally identifiable information (PII) from free text. It replaces things like email addresses, phone numbers, names, and credit card numbers with anonymous placeholder tags such as {{EMAIL}} or {{PHONE}}, so you can keep working with the text without exposing the people it mentions.

It is aimed at developers and data teams who need to anonymize logs, support tickets, survey responses, or other unstructured text before storing, sharing, or analyzing it. The library works on plain text and does not require any metadata about the people involved, though it can use extra context when you have it.

Within the PII and security space, scrubadub sits at the pre-processing stage. Its behavior is built from individual Detectors and PostProcessors, so you can turn detectors on or off and add optional plugins (like spaCy or Stanford NER for names, plus an address detector) to match the languages and regions you care about.
features: Detects and removes many PII types out of the box: names, email addresses, phone numbers, URLs, credit card numbers, dates of birth, and more
Replaces detected items with clear placeholder tags such as {{EMAIL}} and {{PHONE}}
Handles region-specific identifiers, including US/GB/CA postal addresses, US SSNs and GB national insurance numbers, GB tax and driving licence numbers
Configurable through individual Detectors and PostProcessors, with localisation support for different languages and regions
Optional plugin packages (scrubadub_address, scrubadub_spacy, scrubadub_stanford) add NER-based name and address detection
Pure Python, installable from PyPI, requiring Python 3.6 or newer

gettingStarted.intro: Getting started is as simple as installing from PyPI and calling scrubadub.clean() on your text.

steps.heading: Install scrubadub
steps.body: Install the core library from PyPI. It requires Python 3.6 or newer.

steps.heading: Clean a piece of text
steps.body: Import the package and pass your text to scrubadub.clean(). Detected personal information is replaced with placeholder tags.
steps.code:
>>> import scrubadub
>>> text = "My cat can be contacted on example@example.com, or 1800 555-5555"
>>> scrubadub.clean(text)
'My cat can be contacted on {{EMAIL}}, or {{PHONE}}'

steps.heading: Add optional detectors
steps.body: For NER-based name and address detection, install the optional plugin packages and follow their documentation. These require additional dependencies.

useCases: Anonymizing support tickets, chat logs, or emails before storing or sharing them
Stripping PII from text datasets prior to analysis or model training
Redacting customer free-text fields (surveys, feedback forms) to meet privacy requirements
Pre-processing documents to remove names, phone numbers, and IDs before publishing or exporting
